name: Critical File Modified followed by Suspicious Activity
notes:
This rule triggers when critical files or directories are modified, followed by a suspicious activity. An attacker may modify files or drop executables to gain initial access to the machine, and also create backdoors.

Note: Edit the "BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint" Building Block (which is a child of "BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical File Modification") with files or directories to monitor. These locations should be related to boot, backup, logging, or credentials, which have a higher severity when exploited.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 24 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical File Modification match
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 24 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical File Modification match
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 24 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical File Modification match
TD_list: APPLY Critical File Modified followed by Suspicious Activity on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match at least 1 times with the same Machine Identifier (custom) in 24 hour(s) after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Critical File Modification match
RED_list: Event name: Critical File Modified followed by Suspicious Activity
Event description: Detects when critical files or directories are modified, followed by a suspicious activity.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
